market_scope.resolution_source: Station-linked source / Weather Underground style whole-degree daily high

historical_window.supporting_window_note: The source file includes 57 deterministic days from 2026-03-15 to 2026-05-10; the last 45 days are used as the primary calibration window.

ensemble.coverage_note: Individual ensemble members are only available on the final trailing days in this archive and should be used as a confirmation layer rather than the sole base forecast.

detection.rule: Default regime when the day is neither clearly free-heating nor clearly capped by cloud, rain or fog.

behavior.sample_note: Only one mixed day appears in the primary 45-day window; do not overfit this regime.
No material foggy sample appears in the spring primary window; treat fog as a modest heating cap until it clearly burns off.

adjustments.name: gfs_warmer_than_ecmwf
adjustments.reason: GFS can add a small upside check when it is materially warmer, but ECMWF remains the Munich base.

adjustments.name: clear_regime
adjustments.reason: Clear Munich days strongly outperform raw model maxima and have large diurnal ranges.

adjustments.name: cloudy_regime
adjustments.reason: Cloudy Munich days cap upside, but ECMWF is already less wrong in this regime.

adjustments.name: rainy_regime
adjustments.reason: Rainy Munich days peak earlier and leave limited afternoon recovery.

adjustments.name: foggy_regime
adjustments.reason: Morning fog is treated as a mild heating cap unless later METARs show clean clearing.

adjustments.name: obs10_below_ecmwf10
adjustments.reason: A true observed lag versus ECMWF is meaningful because ECMWF is already normally cold in Munich mornings.

adjustments.name: noon_underperformance
adjustments.reason: Munich can still add 1-2C after noon, so noon underperformance should be penalized, but less aggressively than in early-peaking cities.

adjustments.name: after_14_no_new_high_and_cooling
adjustments.reason: After Munich enters the 14-15 local peak zone, cooling should reduce higher buckets.

intraday_path_rules.max_by_noon_rule: Munich usually still has 1-2C of upside after noon; 3C is possible and 4C is rare.

morning_model_bias_rule.effect: Do not automatically upgrade the final high; ECMWF is normally cold in Munich mornings.

clear_day_heating_rule.effect: Keep the ECMWF bucket plus one degree alive unless METAR path or cloud development invalidates heating.

rain_after_high_rule.effect: Start locking the current high as primary exact bucket and reduce higher buckets.

sticky_high_rule.effect: Increase confidence that the current high is the final high.

no_new_high_after_15local_rule.condition: time >= 15 local and no new high for at least one hour
no_new_high_after_15local_rule.effect: Discount the next higher bucket aggressively.

trading_rules.post_14local_market_scope: At 14 local and later, current observed high becomes much more important, but 1C additional upside remains possible.
trading_rules.skip_condition: If absolute edge is below threshold, do not trade.

scenario_invalidation.condition: fresh_METAR moves at least 1C against the scenario after 14 local

path_break.condition: expected clear-day heating fails by 14-15 local

post_peak_confirmation.condition: current_high confirmed by repeated METARs and late-day cooling after 15 local

warnings: Do not treat Munich like Madrid; post-noon upside is usually 1-2C, not 3-5C.
Do not overreact to observed 10 local being above ECMWF; ECMWF is normally cold in Munich mornings.
Clear days dominate the primary sample and frequently outperform both ECMWF and GFS.
Cloudy and rainy days reduce upside and tend to peak earlier.
Ensemble member coverage is short, so use p75-p85 only as a realistic upper-bound confirmation layer.

operational_summary.primary_city_filter: Whether the day can maintain clear-sky heating into 14-15 local
operational_summary.main_post_noon_rule: Munich usually adds 1-2C after noon; 3C is possible, 4C is rare.
operational_summary.main_post_peak_rule: After 15 local, repeated failure to print a new high should quickly shift probability toward the current observed high.
